name: Async / Await
def: Syntactic support for writing non-blocking, suspendable computations in sequential style.
problem: Handle massive I/O-bound concurrency without one OS thread per in-flight operation.
mechanism: await suspends a coroutine/state machine, yielding the thread until the awaited future completes; an executor/event loop resumes it.
benefits: Scales to huge I/O concurrency cheaply
Low memory per task vs threads
limits: Debugging suspended state machines is hard
failures: Blocking calls inside async starving the executor
myths: "Async = parallel" — it is concurrency; parallelism needs multiple threads
